description: First test on a real production project. Genome compressed from 6 distributed CLAUDE.md files (~4,700 tokens) to single genome (~1,430 tokens). 3.3x compression.

conditions.no_context: Project name only, zero project knowledge

compression_analysis.no_context_baseline: 39.7% (no-context achieves 39.7% of full context, heavily skewed by Claude anomaly)

details.note: ANOMALY: Claude no-context scored 54/60 despite receiving zero project context. Answers contain project-specific file paths, rule numbers, demo account passwords, and exact form names that cannot be derived from general knowledge. This suggests either (a) the model has memorized this project from training data, (b) context leakage in the experimental setup, or (c) the model fabricated plausible-sounding details that happened to be correct. This result invalidates the no-context baseline for Claude.
Missing Q1, Q2, Q5, Q26 due to JSON parsing issues (scored 0)
Missing Q1, Q2, Q5, Q15, Q26 due to JSON parsing issues (scored 0)
Missing Q1, Q2, Q5, Q26 (scored 0). All other answers were NOT IN CONTEXT, correctly reflecting zero project knowledge.

analysis.headline: Context Genome retains 96.8% accuracy at 3.3x compression on a real production project. The genome is a viable CLAUDE.md replacement.
analysis.accuracy_retention: Across all three models, the genome averaged 50.3/60 vs full context 52.0/60 (96.8% retention). Codex genome actually outscored Codex full (54 vs 53), suggesting the compressed format may reduce noise. Gemini genome lost 4 points vs full (42 vs 46), partly due to an additional missing answer (Q15). Claude genome lost only 2 points (55 vs 57).
analysis.no_context_baseline: Codex and Gemini no-context conditions performed as expected: Codex scored 8/60 (13.3%) with only generic guesses on Q1-Q5 and Q26-Q30; Gemini scored 0/60 answering NOT IN CONTEXT for everything. This confirms project-specific knowledge genuinely requires context.
analysis.claude_anomaly: CRITICAL FINDING: Claude no-context scored 54/60 despite receiving zero project context. This is only 3 points below Claude full (57/60) and higher than Claude genome (55/60). The answers contain exact file paths (universal-html-template.ts, pdf-template-generator.ts), rule numbers (Rule #11, Rule #13), demo account credentials (sarah@ccof.org, password123), form series details (G2.0 Sites & Parcels, H2.0 Products), and Sprint S120 references. This either indicates training data contamination or experimental setup leakage. The Claude no-context result should be disregarded when computing baselines.

category_analysis.critical_rules: Full avg 8.0, Genome avg 8.0, No-context avg 3.0. Genome preserves critical rules perfectly. Gemini lost 4 points to missing answers (JSON parsing failures on Q1, Q2, Q5).
category_analysis.architecture: Full avg 9.3, Genome avg 9.0, No-context avg 2.7. Architecture knowledge transfers nearly perfectly through compression. The 3.3x compression retains structural project knowledge.
category_analysis.patterns: Full avg 8.7, Genome avg 8.3, No-context avg 3.3. UI patterns (CSS classes, component choices) survive compression well. Gemini genome lost Q15 to parsing.
category_analysis.workflows: Full avg 8.7, Genome avg 9.3, No-context avg 3.3. Genome actually OUTPERFORMED full on workflows (Codex genome scored 10/10 vs Codex full 8/10). The compressed format may surface workflow steps more clearly.
category_analysis.cross_cutting: Full avg 7.7, Genome avg 6.3, No-context avg 3.3. Largest compression loss category. Cross-cutting concerns (tracing changes across packages) require more detailed knowledge that compression may strip. Codex genome dropped from 9 to 6 here.
category_analysis.gotchas: Full avg 8.3, Genome avg 9.3, No-context avg 2.7. Genome outperformed full on gotchas, suggesting the compressed format preserves 'don't do this' warnings effectively.

analysis.compression_viability: The genome is clearly viable as a CLAUDE.md replacement. At 3.3x compression (1,430 vs 4,700 tokens), it retains 96.8% accuracy. The compression losses are concentrated in cross-cutting concerns where detailed package-by-package knowledge matters most. Critical rules, architecture, patterns, workflows, and gotchas all compress with minimal loss.
analysis.information_lost_in_compression: Primary losses: (1) Cross-cutting package details — genome answers for Q21/Q22 were less specific about which exact files in each package need changes. (2) Some specific counts/details became vaguer (Q9 V-series meaning, Q24 wizard-only pre-cert detail). (3) Minor edge case details dropped. No critical rules or architectural decisions were lost.
analysis.gemini_parsing_penalty: Gemini lost 8 points (full) and 10 points (genome) to JSON parsing failures that prevented Q1, Q2, Q5, and Q26 from being included. Adjusting for these missing answers, Gemini full would be ~54/60 and Gemini genome ~52/60, more competitive with Codex.
analysis.model_comparison: Claude > Codex > Gemini across all conditions. Claude showed the smallest full-to-genome drop (2 points). Codex was remarkably consistent (genome actually beat full by 1 point). Gemini had the largest absolute scores but was penalized by parsing issues.
